2604.21310.title: Adversarial Evasion in Non-Stationary Malware Detection: Minimizing Drift Signals through Similarity-Constrained Perturbations
2604.21310.paper_summary: Deep learning has emerged as a powerful approach for malware detection, demonstrating impressive accuracy across various data representations. However, these models face critical limitations in real-world, non-stationary environments where both malware characteristics and detection systems continuously evolve. Our research investigates a fundamental security question: Can an attacker generate adversarial malware samples that simultaneously evade classification and remain inconspicuous to drift monitoring mechanisms? We propose a novel approach that generates targeted adversarial examples in the classifier's standardized feature space, augmented with sophisticated similarity regularizers. By carefully constraining perturbations to maintain distributional similarity with clean malware, we create an optimization objective that balances targeted misclassification with drift signal minimization. We quantify the effectiveness of this approach by comprehensively comparing classifier output probabilities using multiple drift metrics. Our experiments demonstrate that similarity constraints can reduce output drift signals, with $\ell_2$ regularization showing the most promising results. We observe that perturbation budget significantly influences the evasion-detectability trade-off, with increased budget leading to higher attack success rates and more substantial drift indicators.
2604.21310.paper_summary_zh: 深度學習已成為一種強大的惡意軟體檢測方法，在各種數據表徵中顯示出令人印象深刻的準確性。<lb/>然而，這些模型在現實世界中面臨著關鍵的限制，特別是在非穩態環境中，惡意軟體特徵和檢測系統都在不斷演變。<lb/>我們的研究探討了一個基本的安全問題：攻擊者能否生成同時避開分類並對漂移監測機制保持不顯眼的對抗性惡意軟體樣本？<lb/>我們提出了一種新穎的方法，在分類器的標準化特徵空間中生成針對性的對抗性範例，並增強了複雜的相似性正則化器。<lb/>通過仔細約束擾動以保持與乾淨惡意軟體的分佈相似性，我們創建了一個優化目標，平衡了針對性錯誤分類與漂移信號最小化。<lb/>我們通過使用多種漂移指標全面比較分類器輸出概率來量化這種方法的有效性。<lb/>我們的實驗表明，相似性約束可以減少輸出漂移信號，其中 $\ell_2$ 正則化顯示出最有前景的結果。<lb/>我們觀察到，擾動預算對逃避可檢測性權衡有顯著影響，預算增加導致更高的攻擊成功率和更明顯的漂移指標。
2604.21310.author: Pawan Acharya et.al.
